use_case: Component-only state that doesn't need sharing
anti_patterns: Prop drilling, lifting state unnecessarily
best_practices: Keep state close to where it's used, use for UI-only state
example_scenarios: Form input values, toggle states, modal open/close

use_case: App-wide UI state like theme, sidebar, notifications
anti_patterns: Using server state tools for UI state, Redux for simple state
best_practices: Use selectors, slice state by feature, persist selectively
example_scenarios: Dark mode, sidebar state, toast notifications, modals

use_case: API data that needs caching, refetching, optimistic updates
anti_patterns: Storing API data in Redux/local state, no cache strategy
best_practices: Use query keys, implement stale-while-revalidate, optimistic updates
example_scenarios: User data, product listings, search results, paginated data

anti_patterns: useState for each field, no validation library
best_practices: Use schema validation (Zod), debounced submission, dirty state tracking
example_scenarios: Multi-step forms, complex validation, file uploads

anti_patterns: Storing filters in local state, ignoring URL
best_practices: Sync with URL, use for filters, pagination, search queries
example_scenarios: Search filters, pagination, sort order, modal state

anti_patterns: Storing sensitive data locally, no size limits
best_practices: Encrypt sensitive data, handle quota, sync with server
example_scenarios: User preferences, offline data, draft content

anti_patterns: Using for long-term storage, not clearing on logout
best_practices: Clear on logout, use for temporary data

anti_patterns: Recalculating unnecessarily, storing duplicates
best_practices: Memoize expensive calculations, use selectors

use_case: Loading, error, success states for async operations
anti_patterns: Manual loading states everywhere, no error handling
best_practices: Use built-in states, implement retry logic, handle edge cases

anti_patterns: Storing tokens in Redux, no refresh logic
best_practices: Use httpOnly cookies, implement refresh, clear on logout

anti_patterns: Not persisting, flash of wrong theme
best_practices: Use CSS variables, persist preference, prevent flash

anti_patterns: Storing route in Redux, not using router
best_practices: Use router for navigation, URL for shareable state

anti_patterns: No undo capability, losing drafts
best_practices: Implement undo stack, auto-save drafts
example_scenarios: Form editing, content editor, drawing app

anti_patterns: Polling when WebSocket available, no reconnection
best_practices: Use subscriptions, handle reconnection, optimistic updates

use_case: Immediate UI update before server confirmation
anti_patterns: No rollback on error, not updating cache
best_practices: Implement rollback, update cache immediately

anti_patterns: Loading all data, not caching pages
best_practices: Use infinite scroll, cache pages, sync with URL

anti_patterns: Storing entire objects instead of IDs
best_practices: Store IDs, derive selected items, clear on action

anti_patterns: State for interval ID, memory leaks
best_practices: Cleanup on unmount, use refs for IDs

anti_patterns: Not using dedicated library, complex manual state
best_practices: Use established libraries, handle reordering

anti_patterns: Silent failures, no error boundaries
best_practices: Use error boundaries, log errors, show user feedback
example_scenarios: API errors, component errors, validation errors
